compiled_state.description:
# gitleaks false-positive on a docs example secret blocks docs changes (+ 189 unblock)

## What
gitleaks' generic-api-key rule flags a DOCUMENTED throwaway placeholder in docs
(docs/llm-framework.md:311 `export LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY=sk-lf-1234567890`), and because the
secret detector scans the whole CHANGED FILE, any edit to that file (e.g. change 189) is
force-BLOCKed. Fix the false-positive class without weakening detection of genuine secrets.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Add a `.gitleaks.toml` allowlist scoped to documented placeholder patterns (sk-lf-/pk-lf- throwaways; docs example keys) — NOT a blanket **/*.md exclusion (a real doc secret must still block).
- [ ] Fix the misleading docs/llm-framework.md:311 placeholder to an obviously-non-secret form.
- [ ] The secret detector still fail-closes on a genuine secret (a test/fixture proves a real pattern still matches).
- [ ] This sits at/before change 189 in the Gerrit stack so 189's re-review passes gitleaks; make lint green; commit carries a rebar-ticket trailer.
compiled_state.title: gitleaks false-positive on docs example secret blocks docs changes
